title: Assess coupling and cohesion before changing a module
description: Map a module's responsibilities and its inbound/outbound dependencies to judge whether it is cohesive and loosely coupled before reshaping it.
body: Steps: 1. List the module's responsibilities and check they form one coherent purpose (cohesion). 2. Enumerate inbound (fan-in) and outbound (fan-out) dependencies. 3. Flag high fan-out to volatile modules and low cohesion (unrelated responsibilities bundled together). 4. Decide whether to split low-cohesion modules or narrow couplings before adding to them. 5. Record the assessment as evidence. Rollback point: before splitting or merging modules. Validation: the target module has a single clear responsibility and dependencies justified by that purpose; piling new behavior onto an already low-cohesion module increases change cost and is the wrong move.
